zh.lifeSummary: 當Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne於1092年出生在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯姆洛斯時，他的父親Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford27歲，母親Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont28歲。他於大約1130年與Adeline de Rollos結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1172年在英國林可爾史爾享年80歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦堂爾 哈姆勒次波普拉爾哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne于1092年出生在法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯姆洛斯时，他的父亲Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford27岁，母亲Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont28岁。他于大约1130年与Adeline de Rollos结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1172年在英国林可爾史爾享年80岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦堂爾 哈姆勒次波普拉爾哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. In the Middle Ages it was a comparatively common name, which gave rise to a surname. It was borne by the Norman crusader Baldwin of Boulogne, who in 1100 was elected first king of Jerusalem, and by four further crusader kings of Jerusalem. It continued to be used by some families in England into the 17th century, but in modern times it normally represents a transferred use of the surname rather than a direct revival of the Norman given name.

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne was born in 1092, in Meulles, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France, his father, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, was 27 and his mother, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, was 28. He married Adeline de Rollos about 1130. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1172, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in All Saints Churchyard, Poplar, Tower Hamlets, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne est né en 1092, à Meulles, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Adeline de Rollos environ 1130. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1172, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à All Saints Churchyard, Poplar, Tower Hamlets, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourneが1092年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスメウレスで生まれた時，父Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertfordは27歳，母Adelize en Beauvais de Clermontは28歳であった。約1130年にAdeline de Rollosと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1172年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで80歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドントウエル ハムレツポプラーアル サインツ チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints Churchyard, Chelsea Royal Bor: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 타워 함레츠 포플라 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne(이)가 1092년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 우레스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont(은)는 28세였다. 그는 대략 1130년 Adeline de Rollos(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1172년 영국 링코실르에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 타워 함레츠 포플라 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne nasceu em 1092, em Meulles, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Adeline de Rollos aproximadamente 1130. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1172, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em All Saints Churchyard, Poplar, Tower Hamlets, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.All Saints Churchyard, Chelsea Royal Bor: алл сеинц чурчярд, поплар, тоуир хамлец, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne родился в 1092 г. в местности меуллесь, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Adeline de Rollos около 1130 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1172 г. в местности линколншир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц чурчярд, поплар, тоуир хамлец, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baldwin Fitz Gilbert de Clare lord of Bourne nació en 1092, en Meulles, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Adeline de Rollos aproximadamente 1130. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1172, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en All Saints Churchyard, Poplar, Tower Hamlets, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
